notes: IT industry rush to SOA 'decidedly underway'; 90% of companies to exit 2006 with SOA activities at least underway; many large enterprises with >1 year development experience.

Major enterprise service management platform; cross-platform product used beyond HP hardware customers; operations-focused; integration cycle with Mercury over next 18 months; capabilities: Application Management, Asset Management, BSM, Config Mgmt, Event/Perf Mgmt, Service Desk, Identity Mgmt, IT Compliance/Governance, IT Service Mgmt, Network Service Mgmt, SOA.
Widely used to test and monitor complicated enterprise applications, infrastructure, and client-server performance; Mercury's flagship product.
SOA service registry; Mercury acquired Systinet January 2006; registry integration with OpenView key to HP's SOA management proposition; competitor to IBM, CA, and other ESM players.

Mercury IT Governance Center complements HP OpenView; covers regulatory compliance (SOX 404), policy compliance, IT asset alignment with business strategy; Aberdeen recommends IT governance for SOA success; market needs considerable education.
IT governance means regulatory compliance (especially SOX 404) to many buyers; Aberdeen survey shows different definitions across buyer segments.
ESM market dominated by IBM Tivoli and CA; HP-Mercury aims to improve competitive position; HP already one of major ESM suppliers with OpenView.
Introduction of Windows Vista and Vista Server cited as distraction for HP's 18-month OpenView-Mercury integration cycle — customers distracted by Vista introduction.

Aberdeen survey shows mainframes heavily involved in enterprise-size ($1B+) company SOA plans; HP can monitor zOS remotely but has not committed to deeper mainframe penetration — limiting HP's appeal to large mixed environments.
